findings.title: Scheduler metadata handoff is not executable and mixes snake_case plan fields with camelCase helper contracts

source_refs.value: Chunk 2 public interface: `export interface RawChunkMetadata { id?: string; specSections?: string[]; declaredFiles?: string[]; dependsOn?: string[]; exclusiveResources?: string[]; }`
Chunk 3 public interface: `the architect's plan output now requires, per chunk, a `declared_files:` block, a `depends_on:` block, and (where applicable) `exclusive_resources:``
Chunk 4 rewrite: `Compute waves once: validate plan metadata via `validatePlanMetadata` (Chunk 2); `ok:false` → `PLAN_GAP` to architect. Then `computeWaves({ chunks, concurrencyCap })` (Chunk 1).`

findings.rationale: The external plan contract is snake_case YAML, while the TypeScript validator/scheduler contracts are camelCase objects, and the plan never assigns a parser, normalizer, or exact runnable command for the coordinator to get from `plan.md` blocks to `RawChunkMetadata[]` or `ChunkNode[]`. A builder implementing exactly this plan must invent the most safety-critical handoff, which can make valid plans fail validation or let the coordinator do model-mediated metadata extraction despite the stated machine-readable/deterministic requirement.
findings.acceptance_check: `npx vitest run scripts/build-scheduler/__tests__/parsePlanMetadata.test.ts` passes with a fixture containing `declared_files:` and `depends_on:` and asserting the normalized `ChunkNode` has `declaredFiles` and `dependsOn`; `.claude/agents/feature-coordinator.md` contains an exact scheduler invocation command found by `grep -n "computeWaves" .claude/agents/feature-coordinator.md`.
findings.operator_decision_required_reason: This changes the core scheduler substrate and cross-chunk metadata contract before the coordinator can safely consume it.

findings.title: Merge-back contract assumes a git merge from a worktree branch even though builders never commit

source_refs.value: Chunk 4 serialised merge-back step: `Merge the worktree branch into the feature branch.`
Chunk 4 serialised merge-back step: `Coordinator-owned writes on the feature branch ... per-chunk commit (standard message)`
Chunk 6 builder note: `you still never commit, run scoped G1 on your touched files, and report `Files changed`.`

findings.rationale: A git worktree with uncommitted builder changes is not a mergeable branch result. The plan preserves the rule that builders never commit, but then tells the coordinator to merge the worktree branch before making the per-chunk commit. Without an explicit transfer primitive such as patch application, temporary coordinator-owned integration commits with squash, or another named mechanism, conflict detection, commit integrity, and the one-commit-per-chunk guarantee are underspecified.
findings.acceptance_check: `grep -n "git diff --binary\|git apply --3way\|temporary integration commit" .claude/agents/feature-coordinator.md` returns the selected merge-back mechanism, and the same Step 6 section names the base ref and conflict-abort command.
findings.operator_decision_required_reason: The merge-back mechanism is the central data-integrity primitive for parallel construction and has architectural blast radius.

findings.title: The first-three-builds rollout gate has no persistent counter or ownership contract

source_refs.value: Chunk 4 rollout gate: `parallel path is opt-in for the first 3 builds via an operator phrase ... absent the phrase, force concurrency=1 ... After 3 builds, default flips to on`
§6 decision F: `Parallel path is opt-in for the first 3 builds via an operator phrase. Spec suggests `launch feature coordinator parallel`.`

findings.rationale: The plan changes runtime defaults based on a cross-build count, but it does not say where that count lives, who increments it, how resumes are distinguished from new builds, or how consuming repos know the default has flipped after sync. A coordinator executing the prompt later cannot implement `first 3 builds` deterministically.
findings.acceptance_check: `grep -n "rollout.*count\|parallel.*first 3" .claude/agents/feature-coordinator.md` shows the durable state source/path and the actor responsible for incrementing it.

findings.title: `serialisedReasons` dependency semantics contradict the algorithm and lack a test

source_refs.value: Chunk 1 algorithm: `Record a `serialisedReasons` entry whenever a chunk is forced into a separate wave for overlap/resource/cap.`
§4 Build parallelism: ``serialisedReasons` this plan would emit: `{chunkId:'2', reason:'exclusive-resource', conflictsWith:'1'}`, `{chunkId:'6', reason:'dependency'}`.`

findings.rationale: The public result type and plan-level example say dependency serialisation is reported, but the algorithm only records overlap, resource, and cap splits. A builder can implement either behavior and still point to a plan quote, producing unstable audit output for the coordinator.
findings.acceptance_check: `npx vitest run scripts/build-scheduler/__tests__/computeWaves.test.ts -t dependency` passes and asserts a dependent chunk produces a `serialisedReasons` entry with `{ reason: 'dependency' }`.

findings.title: The final G2 section still tells executors to run `npx tsx` despite the resolved Vitest decision

source_refs.value: §1.5 verification model: `Unit tests for the pure modules via Vitest — `npx vitest run scripts/build-scheduler/``
§8 G2 / end-of-construction: `The local end-of-construction check here is limited to: the four `npx tsx` unit-test runs (Chunks 1, 2) and the `manifest.json` JSON-validity parse`

findings.rationale: The detailed body resolves the runner to Vitest, but the end-of-construction summary still names `npx tsx` and says there are four unit-test runs. Builders and reviewers often use the final verification section first; this stale summary can send them to the wrong local gate.
findings.acceptance_check: `grep -n "npx tsx unit-test\|four .*unit-test" tasks/builds/parallel-worktree-builders/plan.md` returns no matches, and §8 contains both `npx vitest run scripts/build-scheduler/__tests__/computeWaves.test.ts` and `npx vitest run scripts/build-scheduler/__tests__/validatePlanMetadata.test.ts`.

findings.title: Prose-only chunks name grep anchors but provide no chunk-local grep gates

source_refs.value: Chunk 4 test considerations: `deterministic grep (A7) — Step 6 must contain anchors for: wave loop, runtime independence gate, concurrent builder dispatch with `isolation: "worktree"`, serialised merge-back, `INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION` merge-back guard, concurrency=1 fallback.`

findings.rationale: Chunks 3, 4, and 5 are judgement-heavy prose edits, but the plan does not give the builder or reviewer concrete local commands to prove the required anchors landed. It says grep is the deterministic evidence, then defers it to branch review/doc-sync without adding the grep commands to either the owning chunks or Chunk 6.
findings.acceptance_check: Plan chunk verification blocks include these literal commands: `grep -n "declared_files" .claude/agents/architect.md`, `grep -n "INDEPENDENCE_VIOLATION" .claude/agents/feature-coordinator.md`, and `grep -n "under-declared" .claude/agents/claude-plan-review.md .claude/agents/chatgpt-plan-review.md`.
